# 08 — PWA & Offline Support

## Overview

The frontend is a Progressive Web App using `@ducanh2912/next-pwa` (Workbox-based). It supports installation on mobile/desktop and provides offline caching.

---

## Manifest

**File**: `public/manifest.json`

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| App name | Qraft |
| Display | standalone |
| Categories | education, games, entertainment |
| Icons | Multiple resolutions (iOS, Android, Windows 11 tiles) |

---

## Service Worker Strategy

**File**: `public/sw.js` (auto-generated by next-pwa)

| Resource Type | Strategy | Details |
|---------------|----------|---------|
| Pages & APIs | NetworkFirst | Fresh content preferred, falls back to cache |
| Stylesheets | StaleWhileRevalidate | Serve cached, update in background |
| Fonts (Google) | CacheFirst | 1-year expiration |
| Images | CacheFirst | Long-lived cache |
| JS bundles | CacheFirst | Immutable Next.js chunks |
| Audio/Video | CacheFirst | `RangeRequestsPlugin` for seeking support |

### Excluded from caching
- `/api/auth/callback` (auth redirects must be live)

---

## Next.js PWA Config

```javascript
// next.config.js
withPWA({
  dest: "public",
  disable: process.env.NODE_ENV === "development",  // Disabled in dev
  precaching: true,
  reloadOnOnline: true,
})
```

---

## Lifecycle

- **skipWaiting**: New service worker activates immediately
- **clientsClaim**: Takes control of all open tabs on activation
- **Precaching**: All Next.js static chunks and built assets cached at install time
- **Online reload**: Page reloads automatically when connectivity returns
